items.paper_statement: The cardinality of the sample vertex set imported from the first smoke-test paper.
items.search_text: synthetic downstream smoke test imported sample vertex set cardinality
items.notes: Definition exported to show that a downstream paper can expose values built from an upstream paper package.

items.paper_statement: The imported sample cardinality is three.
items.search_text: synthetic downstream smoke test reuse cardinality theorem imported paper
items.notes: Direct reuse of the first smoketest paper's cardinality theorem.

items.paper_statement: The imported sample vertex set unioned with any right set is contained in itself.
items.lean_statement: SmokeImportPaper.Internal.sampleVertexSet union right subset SmokeImportPaper.Internal.sampleVertexSet union right
items.search_text: synthetic downstream smoke test finite union subset reuse imported theorem
items.notes: Exercises an upstream theorem with an explicit downstream argument.

items.paper_statement: Doubling inside the finite range sum over the imported sample cardinality equals doubling the whole sum.
items.search_text: synthetic downstream smoke test finite sum range double imported theorem mathlib
items.notes: Uses an upstream big-operator theorem at an imported cardinality.

items.paper_statement: The doubled range sum over the imported sample cardinality evaluates to six.
items.search_text: synthetic downstream smoke test imported cardinality finite sum evaluates six
items.notes: Combines upstream reuse with a small downstream computation.
